summary: The July 9 scout emphasizes finite-temperature QCD matter composition, QCD critical-end-point sensitivity to Fierz-complete four-quark channels, ALICE- and STAR-facing femtoscopy and fluctuation observables, robust impact-parameter reconstruction with cross-model ML checks, and physics AI/ML methods for lattice-field-theory interpretability, nuclear fusion cross-section uncertainty, and HEP data-preservation workflows.
top_takeaway: arXiv:2607.07140 and arXiv:2607.07354 are the strongest heavy-ion theory picks because they address QCD matter composition and the critical-end-point location with methods directly relevant to heavy-ion equation-of-state modeling.

papers.title: Hadronic and partonic composition of QCD matter across the crossover
papers.authors_short: Artemiy Lysenko, Mark I. Gorenstein, Marek Gazdzicki, Roman Poberezhniuk, Volodymyr Vovchenko
papers.abstract: Constructs a lattice-QCD-constrained equation of state with hadronic and partonic components connected by a smooth crossover, finding a gradual transition in which hadronic states remain important above the chiral pseudocritical temperature.

papers.title: Fierz-complete four-quark interactions and the QCD phase diagram
papers.authors_short: Zi-ning Wang, Li-jun Zhou, Chuang Huang, Rui Wen, Shi Yin, Wei-jie Fu
papers.abstract: Uses functional renormalization group methods to study how Fierz-complete four-quark interactions modify the finite-temperature and finite-density QCD phase diagram, including the critical-end-point location.

papers.title: Kaon-deuteron correlation function from an effective field theory approach
papers.abstract: Computes K-d and K+d femtoscopic correlation functions using unitarized chiral EFT amplitudes, Faddeev treatments, multiple scattering, and Coulomb effects, then compares with ALICE Pb-Pb and high-multiplicity pp data.

papers.title: Recent highlights from the STAR Experiment
papers.abstract: Summarizes recent STAR results on mean-pT correlations, net-proton cumulants, identical-pion femtoscopy, baryon-strangeness correlations, and baryon-baryon femtoscopy across sqrt(sNN)=3-200 GeV.

papers.title: Machine learning the impact parameter in heavy-ion collisions at sqrt(sNN)=4 and 11 GeV: a cross-check study with UrQMD, AMPT, and JAM
papers.authors_short: Xiaoqing Yue, Guojun Wei, Yongjia Wang, Zhilong Li, Pengcheng Li, et al.
papers.abstract: Uses supervised and unsupervised ML to reconstruct impact parameter and centrality classes in Au+Au collisions at 4 and 11 GeV, explicitly cross-checking generalization across UrQMD, AMPT, and JAM transport models.

papers.title: Single inclusive hadron and jet production in lepton-hadron scattering
papers.abstract: Presents a joint QCD+QED factorization calculation for large-pT single inclusive hadron and jet production in lepton-hadron scattering, including lepton distribution functions and predictions for JLab and EIC energies.

papers.title: Extracting Barrier Distributions from Fusion Cross Sections
papers.abstract: Benchmarks Gaussian processes and Bayesian neural-network inference for extracting fusion barrier distributions from noisy and sparse cross-section data, with uncertainty quantification and an open-source implementation.

papers.title: Weight-Space Physics: Interpretable Hypernetworks for Lattice Quantum Field Theories
papers.authors_short: Tobias Goebel, Julian R. Ebelt, Zier Mensch, Mathis Gerdes, Miranda C. N. Cheng
papers.abstract: Introduces a hypernetwork/JEPA-style weight generator for normalizing-flow samplers in lattice field theory, showing that learned weight-space structure can recover phase-transition information and interpolate to unseen couplings.

papers.title: Data Preservation in High Energy Physics: Global Report 2026
papers.authors_short: Matthew Bellis, Jamie Boyd, Daniel Britzger, Andy Buckley, Chris Burr, et al.
papers.abstract: Reports DPHEP data-preservation progress since 2024, including automation, AI/ML for data curation, metadata extraction, workflow optimization, open data, and FAIR-principle adoption.

papers.title: A Quiet Failure in Calibrated Virtual Screening: Marginal Conformal Prediction Under-Covers the Minority Class, and a Class-Conditional Fix Recovers It
papers.authors_short: Muhammadjon Tursunbadalov, Mustafojon Tursunbadalov, et al.
papers.abstract: Shows that marginal conformal prediction can satisfy global coverage while badly under-covering minority classes on imbalanced datasets, and that class-conditional conformal prediction restores per-class reliability.

archive_notes.backfill: The run included a planned homepage backfill for 2026-07-08 using existing metadata because that day was present in data/reports but absent from index.html before the July 9 run.
